seoTitle: How to Create Roadmaps in Jira: Step-by-Step
metaDescription: Build a visual project roadmap in Jira by creating epics, scheduling timelines, and mapping dependencies so your team stays aligned on priorities.
intro: Keeping strategic plans separate from daily sprint work makes it hard to see how tasks connect to bigger goals. This guide covers building a Jira roadmap from scratch — creating epics, scheduling them on a timeline, and linking dependencies between related work.

steps.heading: Open the roadmap view
steps.body: From your software project's sidebar, select the Timeline option to reveal the interactive roadmap board where planning happens.

steps.heading: Create your epics
steps.body: Add major feature buckets as epics, giving each one a clear title and a distinct color so they're easy to tell apart on the roadmap.

steps.heading: Schedule the timeline
steps.body: Drag across the calendar grid to set start and due dates for each epic, mapping out roughly when that work should happen.

steps.heading: Link nested tasks and dependencies
steps.body: Add child tasks under each epic, and draw connections between epics to flag which pieces of work are blocking or depend on others.

steps.heading: Share your roadmap
steps.body: Use the view filters to focus on assignee or progress status, then share or export the roadmap for stakeholder updates.

faq.question: Where do I find the roadmap feature in Jira?
faq.answer: Open your software project and select the Timeline option from the left-hand sidebar to access the roadmap view.

faq.question: What's the difference between an epic and a task on a Jira roadmap?
faq.answer: Epics act as the larger containers that group related tasks and stories together, and they're what's typically shown as bars on the roadmap timeline.

faq.question: Can I show dependencies between epics on a roadmap?
faq.answer: Yes, you can draw a connection between two epics to flag a blocking relationship or sequencing constraint directly on the timeline.

faq.question: Can I share my Jira roadmap with people outside the project?
faq.answer: You can filter the roadmap view and share a snapshot or link so stakeholders can see progress without digging into the full board.
